summary: RE-TEST iteration 23: verified the inert-wrapper fix for read-only Scene RECALL and the collision-proof scene IDs. End-to-end Playwright run executed every step of the brief (capture, mute, recall in read-only, mutation guards, capture guard, rapid-capture uniqueness, full regression incl. reload persistence). All 10 steps PASSED.
critical_code_review_comments: SceneBar.jsx still has no outside-click dismissal for the right-click menu (relies on onMouseLeave only). Cosmetic, not blocking — flagged in prior iterations.

DSPApp.jsx wraps SelectedChannelPanel/InputsRow/ChannelStrips with `inert` but SceneBar is intentionally outside that wrapper — this matches the fix intent; consider adding an inline code comment in DSPApp.jsx noting the deliberate placement to prevent future regressions.

dspStore.js createScene uses Date.now()+random(1e6) which is robust for UI burst clicks; still strictly possible (≈1/1M) to collide if two captures land in the same ms. Consider crypto.randomUUID() for absolute uniqueness. Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: Test created 6 scenes in localStorage (TEST_Renamed among them); ephemeral — gone on next clear.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 23 fully validates iteration 22's outstanding bug (read-only recall) and the random-suffix scene IDs. Verified flows: (a) lastRecalledSceneId persisted to localStorage matches scene id, (b) scene-live-{id} indicator becomes visible on recalled slot, (c) inputs[0].mute correctly flips to false then true when recalling Scene 1 then Scene 2, (d) mutating menu buttons carry disabled attribute and store guard rejects clicks (scenes JSON unchanged), (e) empty-slot capture button disabled in readonly (scene count stays at 2), (f) rapid 4-click capture produces 6 unique scene ids, (g) rename / overwrite / delete all work after unlock; updatedAt added on overwrite; persistence verified after page reload.
rca of the issue: Iteration 22 issue was caused by <div inert={readOnly||undefined}> wrapping SceneBar in DSPApp.jsx — `inert` blocks ALL pointer events on descendants including the recall button. Fix: moved SceneBar outside the inert wrapper; mutating buttons retain individual `disabled={readOnly}` guards plus reducer-level guard via `guard()` in dspStore.js. Verified the recall button is now clickable and reducer recallScene path is unguarded by design (analogous to loadPresetState). Scene ID collision was avoided by appending Math.floor(Math.random()*1e6) to Date.now().
